ddls:
CREATE TABLE "bus_ridership" (
  "ridership_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ridership record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the ridership data */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time of the ridership data */,
  "dayofweek" INTEGER /* Day of the week (1-7) corresponding to the ridership data */,
  "bus_cnt" INTEGER /* Number of buses in operation at the given time */,
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the bus route */,
  "trip_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the bus trip */,
  "stop_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the bus stop */,
  "passenger_cnt" INTEGER /* Number of passengers on the bus */,
  "fare_revenue" REAL /* Fare revenue generated by the bus */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ridership_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bus_ridership_date FOREIGN KEY ("date") REFERENCES weather_data ("date"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bus_ridership_date FOREIGN KEY ("date") REFERENCES holiday_data ("date"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bus_ridership_date FOREIGN KEY ("date") REFERENCES temperature_data ("date"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bus_ridership_route_id FOREIGN KEY ("route_id") REFERENCES bus_routes ("route_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bus_ridership_stop_id FOREIGN KEY ("stop_id") REFERENCES bus_stops ("stop_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bus_ridership_trip_id FOREIGN KEY ("trip_id") REFERENCES bus_trips ("trip_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the weather data */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time of the weather data */,
  "weatherA_val" INTEGER /* Value of weather condition A */,
  "weatherB_val" INTEGER /* Value of weather condition B */,
  "weatherPeriod" INTEGER /* Weather period */,
  "weatherE" INTEGER /* Additional weather information */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity percentage */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed in km/h */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Precipitation amount in mm */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "temperature_data" (
  "temperature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each temperature record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the temperature data */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time of the temperature data */,
  "temperatureA" REAL /* Value of temperature A */,
  "temperatureB" REAL /* Value of temperature B */,
  "temperaturePeriod" REAL /* Temperature period */,
  "temperatureE" REAL /* Additional temperature information */,
  "temperature_high" REAL /* High temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "temperature_low" REAL /* Low temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("temperature_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "holiday_data" (
  "holiday_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each holiday record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the holiday */,
  "holiday" INTEGER /* Holiday information */,
  "holiday_name" TEXT /* Name of the holiday */,
  "holiday_type" TEXT /* Type of holiday (e.g. federal, state, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("holiday_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bus_schedules" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bus schedule */,
  "bus_cnt" INTEGER /* Number of buses in operation during the schedule */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the schedule */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the schedule */,
  "schedule_type" TEXT /* Type of schedule (e.g. weekday, weekend, etc.) */,
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the bus route */,
  "trip_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the bus trip */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bus_schedules_end_date FOREIGN KEY ("end_date") REFERENCES bus_ridership ("date"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bus_schedules_route_id FOREIGN KEY ("route_id") REFERENCES bus_routes ("route_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bus_schedules_trip_id FOREIGN KEY ("trip_id") REFERENCES bus_trips ("trip_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bus_schedules_start_date FOREIGN KEY ("start_date") REFERENCES bus_ridership ("date")
)

CREATE TABLE "bus_routes" (
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bus route */,
  "route_name" TEXT /* Name of the bus route */,
  "route_description" TEXT /* Description of the bus route */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("route_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bus_trips" (
  "trip_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bus trip */,
  "trip_name" TEXT /* Name of the bus trip */,
  "trip_description" TEXT /* Description of the bus trip */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trip_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bus_stops" (
  "stop_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bus stop */,
  "stop_name" TEXT /* Name of the bus stop */,
  "stop_description" TEXT /* Description of the bus stop */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stop_id")
)
